goal: Finalize the CardStats Prisma and Next.js application-backend baseline before implementation.
completed: Created the portable Core harness surfaces and pre-coding decision gate.
Validated cold-start context, structural integrity, context budgets, and stale-state conflict detection.
Updated the proposed stack to one Next.js request-plane backend, Prisma ORM/Migrate, PostgreSQL-native SQL where required, and one isolated ingestion worker.
Added repository ignore rules that keep local environment files and credentials out of Git.
nextActions: Rotate the exposed database credential, then founder reviews and approves or revises the updated product contract and ADR 0001.
decisions: Use the Core harness profile only; no hooks, MCP servers, event database, semantic index, daemon, or parallel-agent orchestration.
Keep the harness pre-operational until an authorized representative vertical slice and resume tests pass.
Require ui-ux-pro-max before any visual or interaction design, implementation, refactor, or review task.
Use Next.js as the only MVP request-plane application backend; retain a separate worker for ingestion, Playwright, schedules, and retries.
Use Prisma ORM/Migrate for normal data access and migration orchestration while PostgreSQL constraints and reviewed SQL remain authoritative.
openQuestions: Which countries and inclusion boundary define the first release?
Are the updated product contract and ADR 0001 accepted as the implementation baseline?
Which PostgreSQL provider, TLS/network policy, runtime pooler, hosting region, processors, recovery objectives, browser baseline, and legal review scope are approved?

passed: python3 scripts/harness/check.py — passed manifest, state, link, budget, and credential-pattern checks on branch docs/prisma-next-backend.
python3 scripts/harness/verify.py — passed script syntax, compact context contract, state consistency, and Git whitespace checks.
Bundled evaluate_context_budget.py — all five budgets passed; startup sources remain below 10.5 KB and approximately 2,700 tokens.
Stack consistency search — no remaining Drizzle recommendation or obsolete Next.js BFF wording found in README/docs.
Secret hygiene — .env is ignored and absent from Git status; its contents were not read, staged, tested, or copied.
notRun: Representative product vertical slice: product implementation is not authorized or present.
Cross-client portability: only the current Codex environment is available for this run.
CI/review delivery: no CI or review artifact is configured.

risks: The database credential disclosed in chat must be rotated before use; the repository does not store or validate it.
The remote PostgreSQL endpoint is unapproved until TLS, restricted network access, pooling, least-privilege roles, backups, monitoring, and restore are verified.
The remaining product and architecture decisions require explicit founder approval before the pre-coding gate passes.
CardStats has an active-name collision identified during naming research; trademark and domain clearance remain required.
doNotRepeat: Do not start product coding merely because the blueprint recommends a stack.
Do not add automatic memory, hooks, services, or specialist data stores without a measured need and explicit trust.
Do not connect to, print, stage, or reuse the disclosed database credential; rotate it first.
